data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

4-bucket per parent criterion:
- AC3 ("dual outbound applier paths collapsed to one production path; retired path deleted") — FULLY covered by THIS child (33d0 AC1: exactly one applier path in production, retired path deleted; 33d0 AC2: single multi-sub-op UPDATE through production path asserting each sub-op took effect).
- SC2 ("Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path") — FULLY covered by THIS child (33d0 AC1/AC2).
- AC1 + SC1 (schema SSOT; producer/consumer can't drift on shape) — covered by named siblings 8937 (AC1 _snapshot_schema.py SSOT, AC2 fetch_snapshot validated) + fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake shape honesty). FULLY at roster level.
- AC2 (one round-trip contract fetcher→snapshot→differs→applier through production path, real fixtures) — covered by sibling 8937 AC3 + fe57 fixtures. FULLY at roster level.
- AC4 + SC3 (sub-op telemetry surfaced; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) — covered by sibling 2359 AC1/AC2. FULLY at roster level.
- AC5 + SC4 (hermetic bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip; every "expected-bug" probe guard de-encoded) — covered by sibling 822a AC1/AC2/AC3. FULLY at roster level.
- SC5 (new tests assert semantic round-trip/structure-type; zero change-detector tests) — anti-change-detector POSTURE clause covered across 33d0 AC4, 2359 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3. FULLY (posture clause).
- AC7 (all child stories closed; suite green; no net increase in brittle tests) — structural/aggregate epic roll-up; not a per-child deliverable.

This child's declared OUT-of-scope (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 33d0 itself; its owned AC3/SC2 are fully covered.

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already raised by Iso Tester on siblings 33d0/2359/8937/822a/59ef — restated here only for this pairing's record after re-checking the whole roster INCLUDING this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child — including this one (33d0 AC4) — only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 33d0 AC4 "The combined test asserts effects (sub-op outcomes), not interaction/mock-call-counts" is self-scoped to THIS story's own combined test; 2359 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 33d0 itself for its owned criteria (AC3/SC2 fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked across the sibling set.
